features.name: Mechanical Handle Locking
features.rationale: Must_be — every certified product delivers physical handle blocking. Primary drivers: Safety (9) blocks unauthorized egress; Reliability (8) zero-power operation survives hotel power events. Stress (7): security officers know exits are mechanically secured.

features.name: Audible Alarm Trigger
features.rationale: Must_be — commodity baseline; no product variant ships without it. Safety (9): immediate deterrence; Reliability (7): C2-compliant mechanical trigger at 0V. Stress deliberately suppressed to 5: 95 dB alarm disturbs guests on upper floors.

features.name: Single-Motion Emergency Release
features.rationale: Must_be — DIN EN 179/1125 regulatory mandate. Safety (10): direct fire evacuation compliance. Skill (8) + Stress (8): unfamiliar hotel guests escape without prior knowledge per EN 1125 mandate.

features.name: Key Override Bypass
features.rationale: Must_be — hotel operators expect silent authorized access capability. Stress (7): housekeeping and night audit staff can access without triggering guest-disturbing alarm. Directly addresses E7 false alert fatigue.

features.name: Battery-Powered Operation
features.rationale: Attractive — exceeds commodity FP; operators do not expect IoT monitoring without mains wiring. Cost (8): eliminates conduit installation as dominant retrofit cost driver. Pain (8): removes electrical permits and structural disruption. Delight (7): genuine surprise that IoT monitoring works battery-only.

features.name: Pre-Alarm Warning
features.rationale: Attractive — absent from commodity FP and basic variants. Stress (7): reduces guest disturbance from accidental alarm triggers. Pain (6): eliminates investigation burden from handle-touch incidents. Delight (6): unexpected feature that addresses hotel guest experience sensitivity.

productRelatedNeeds: Minimize the likelihood of incomplete wireless coverage from 30 m transmitter range leaving emergency exits on floors above level 3 without active IoT monitoring
Minimize the time required to design and validate wireless receiver placement to achieve >95% floor coverage before hardware procurement
Minimize the likelihood of 9V battery depletion in high-traffic hotel emergency exits exhausting monitoring capability between scheduled maintenance cycles
Minimize the time required to identify and replace batteries approaching depletion threshold across a 30+ device hotel deployment
Minimize the likelihood of surface-mounted door guardian housing conflicting with hotel brand interior finish specifications
Minimize the effort required to bridge EasyWave event data to hotel BMS platforms running BACnet, KNX, or IP-based protocols
Minimize the likelihood of proprietary wireless protocol dependency preventing multi-brand emergency exit monitoring consolidation
Minimize the likelihood of door-event timestamp and access log data triggering a GDPR DPIA that delays IoT system deployment
